vocab.meaning: cất giữ tiền vào một nơi an toàn (để dành cho tương lai)
vocab.example: Banks provide a secure platform for individuals to tuck away their savings for retirement or unforeseen emergencies.

vocab.meaning: miễn cưỡng phải chi ra một khoản tiền lớn
vocab.example: People often rely on bank loans when they have to fork out a significant amount of money for a new car or home repairs.

vocab.meaning: đổ tiền vào đầu tư (thường là một khoản lớn)
vocab.example: Many investors use specialized bank accounts to sink their capital into diverse portfolios like stocks or gold.

vocab.meaning: giữ lại một phần tiền (không chi tiêu hết)
vocab.example: By using a separate savings account, it's easier to keep back a portion of your paycheck instead of spending it all at once.

vocab.meaning: tiêu một phần tiền từ nguồn tiết kiệm
vocab.example: The advantage of a flexible bank account is that you can dip into your funds whenever a sudden expense arises.
Most employees have their monthly salaries paid in directly to their bank accounts for convenience and safety.

vocab.meaning: làm tăng nợ nần (do chi tiêu quá mức)
vocab.example: Some people use banks poorly and run up massive credit card debts that take years to clear.
In the past, business owners frequently had to write out checks for their suppliers, a process managed entirely by banks.

vocab.meaning: sống dựa vào nguồn tiền nào đó (ví dụ: lãi suất ngân hàng)
vocab.example: High-net-worth individuals often place their wealth in fixed deposits to live off the interest generated each month.

vocab.meaning: giải cứu tài chính (cho ai đó hoặc doanh nghiệp đang gặp khó khăn)
vocab.example: Banks play a crucial role in the economy by providing the necessary credit to bail out struggling businesses during a recession.
